sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.49 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: An automorphism$$of a linearly ordered group is called order preserving\/ if$x < y$implies$x^{ } <y^{ }$. Is it possible to order an abelian strictly isolated normal subgroup of an$S$-group (see Archive 1.47) in such a way that the order is preserved under the action of the inner automorphisms of the whole group? { A.\,I.\,Kokorin} Not always (V.\,V.\,Bludov, Algebra and Logic, { 11} (1972), 341--...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
